content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree containing NNN nodes numbered from 111 to NNN such that the ithi^{th}ith node has a value AiA_iAi​ assigned to it. The root of the tree is node 111.

The power of each node is the **maximum** value assigned to any node present in the subtree of the node.

You are given an integer XXX. Your task is to make the **sum** of the powers of **all** the nodes of the tree **greater than or equal** to XXX. For this, you can perform the following operation:


Choose any node iii (1≤i≤N)(1 \le i \le N)(1≤i≤N) and set Ai:=Ai+1A_i := A_i + 1Ai​:=Ai​+1.
Find the **minimum** number of operations required to make the **sum** of the powers of **all** the nodes of the tree **greater than or equal** to XXX.

Note that, the subtree of a node VVV is the set of all nodes UUU such that VVV lies on the simple path from UUU to the root.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and XXX — the number of nodes and the target sum XXX.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, the values assigned to the nodes.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines describe the edges. The ithi^{th}ith of these N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the minimum number of operations required.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.
It is guaranteed that the given graph is a tree.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Perform 666 operations on node 222 to make the value assigned to it equal to 222222. Thus, power of each node will become 222222 and 87≤22⋅487 \le 22 \cdot 4 87≤22⋅4.
It can be proven that we can not satisfy the condition in less than 666 operations.
Test case 222: Sum of power already satisfies the condition.
